BUGS STILL EXISTING — Developer Action Required (9)
VER-001 HIGH STILL EXISTS
Page: /admin (Sites tab)
Admin Settings > Sites Tab Shows "No rows" — 0 Sites Listed
The Admin Settings > Sites tab displays "No rows" and "0–0 of 0" in pagination, despite the site selector dropdown in the header showing active sites (e.g., "Yxbzjzxj"). The discrepancy means admins cannot manage sites through the admin panel.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login as admin (abhiyant.singh+admin@egalvanic.com)
  2. Navigate to Settings (/admin)
  3. Click the "Sites" tab
  4. Observe: Table shows "No rows" with "0–0 of 0" pagination
  5. Verify: Site selector dropdown in header shows sites are available
Expected: Sites tab should list all active sites matching the site selector dropdown
Actual: "No rows" displayed. 0–0 of 0 pagination. Site data exists but is not loaded into the admin grid.
Impact: Admins cannot view, edit, or manage sites through the admin interface.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /admin (Sites tab)
Suggested Fix: Verify the API endpoint used by the Sites tab fetches the same data as the site selector. Check for missing query parameters or permissions mismatch.
Original Report IDs: PDF-BUG-012, HTML-BUG-020 | First Reported: March 5, 2026 (PDF Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-002 MEDIUM STILL EXISTS
Page: /admin/audit-log
Audit Log Has Duplicate Search Fields
The Mutation Audit Log page renders two separate search fields: one "Search..." above the filter dropdowns (Status, Entity Type, Time Range), and a second "Search items..." in the MUI DataGrid toolbar below. Users face confusion about which search to use.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login as admin
  2. Navigate to Admin > Audit Log (/admin/audit-log)
  3. Observe TWO search fields: "Search..." (top) and "Search items..." (below filters)
Expected: A single, unified search field
Actual: Two search fields visible — one custom, one from MUI DataGrid toolbar. Different styling and placement.
Impact: UX confusion. Users don't know which search is authoritative.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /admin/audit-log
Suggested Fix: Either hide the MUI DataGrid built-in search toolbar or remove the custom search field. Consolidate into one.
Original Report IDs: PDF-BUG-031, HTML-BUG-029 | First Reported: March 5, 2026 (PDF Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-003 HIGH STILL EXISTS
Page: /locations
Building Name Concatenation Bug on Locations Page
Multiple buildings on the Locations page display names that appear to be concatenated — the original name and an updated name joined together. Example: "aSmokeTest_Building_1774442644307_Updated". Additionally, test data buildings created by automation (SmokeTest_Building_*) are polluting the locations list.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login as admin
  2. Navigate to Locations (/locations)
  3. Observe building names in the left panel
  4. Look for entries like "aSmokeTest_Building_177444264..." with "_Updated" suffix
Expected: Each building shows its current name only. No test data visible in production.
Actual: Building names concatenated (old + new). At least 7 SmokeTest buildings visible. Two "DoubleTap-6799" duplicates also present.
Impact: Data integrity issue. Building names are corrupted. Test data pollutes production view.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /locations
Suggested Fix: Fix the update mutation to REPLACE the name field rather than concatenating. Clean up test data from the database.
Original Report IDs: HTML-BUG-010 | First Reported: March 21, 2026 (HTML Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-004 HIGH STILL EXISTS
Page: Tasks, Locations, Assets, Work Orders, Opportunities
Test Data Pollution Across Multiple Pages
Automated test data (SmokeTest_*, AutoTest_*) is visible across the platform. Tasks page shows 5 "SmokeTask_*" entries. Locations shows 7+ "SmokeTest_Building_*" entries. Assets shows "SmokeTest_Asset_*" and "AutoTest_*" entries. Work Orders shows "SmokeTest_WO_*" entries. Opportunities shows "test by kd", "Test121212", "new testt", "edjdjf", "testtt".
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to Tasks (/tasks) — all 5 tasks are SmokeTask_*
  2. Navigate to Locations (/locations) — SmokeTest_Building entries dominate
  3. Navigate to Assets (/assets) — SmokeTest_Asset and AutoTest entries visible
  4. Navigate to Work Orders (/sessions) — SmokeTest_WO entries fill the table
  5. Navigate to Opportunities (/opportunities) — "test by kd", "testtt" etc.
Expected: Only real production data visible to users. Test data should be cleaned up after test runs.
Actual: Automation test artifacts and manual test entries are mixed with production data across every major page.
Impact: Client-facing issue. Damages credibility. Makes real data harder to find. Test data may be triggering incorrect dashboard metrics.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: Tasks, Locations, Assets, Work Orders, Opportunities
Suggested Fix: 1) Implement test data cleanup in automation teardown. 2) Add a cleanup script to remove SmokeTest/AutoTest prefixed entries. 3) Consider a separate test tenant/environment.
Original Report IDs: HTML-BUG-011, PDF-BUG-028, PDF-BUG-029 | First Reported: March 5, 2026 (both reports) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-005 MEDIUM STILL EXISTS
Page: /tasks
All Tasks Overdue — Zero Due Soon, Zero Completed
The Tasks page shows Pending=5, Completed=0, Due Soon (Next 30 Days)=0, Overdue=5. Every single task is overdue (red dates: 25/03/2026). All task names are SmokeTask_* automation artifacts. No real tasks exist in this site.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to Tasks (/tasks)
  2. Observe the summary cards: Pending=5, Completed=0, Due Soon=0, Overdue=5
  3. Note all task Due Dates are 25/03/2026 (red, overdue)
  4. All names are SmokeTask_* automation data
Expected: A reasonable mix of pending, overdue, due soon, and completed tasks. Real task data.
Actual: All 5 tasks are overdue automation artifacts from 25/03/2026. Zero completed. Zero due soon.
Impact: Task management is non-functional for this site. All metrics are skewed by test data.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /tasks
Suggested Fix: Clean up test task data. Ensure automation creates tasks with future due dates or cleans up after runs.
Original Report IDs: HTML-BUG-008 | First Reported: March 21, 2026 (HTML Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-006 LOW STILL EXISTS
Page: /slds
SLDs Page — Duplicate "Select View" Dropdown
The SLDs page renders two "Select View" dropdown buttons: one in the center of the page (as part of the empty state message) and another at the bottom-left of the screen. Both appear to be functional.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to SLDs (/slds)
  2. Observe: Center of page shows "Select a View to Load Assets" with a "Select View" dropdown
  3. Also observe: Bottom-left of page shows another "Select View" dropdown + "Export" button
Expected: A single "Select View" control
Actual: Two "Select View" dropdowns rendered — one in empty state, one in toolbar. Likely a React component rendered in two locations.
Impact: Minor UX confusion. User may not know which to use.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /slds
Suggested Fix: Conditionally render: show the center CTA only when no view is selected, and always show the toolbar dropdown. Or hide the toolbar one when empty state is shown.
Original Report IDs: HTML-BUG-026 | First Reported: March 21, 2026 (HTML Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-007 MEDIUM STILL EXISTS
Page: /any-invalid-url
No 404 Error Page — Invalid URLs Show Blank Page
Navigating to a non-existent URL (e.g., /this-page-does-not-exist-404) renders a completely blank page with only the sidebar navigation. No 404 error message, no "Page Not Found" text, no redirect to dashboard.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login to the application
  2. Navigate to any invalid URL, e.g., https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/this-page-does-not-exist-404
  3. Observe: Content area is completely blank. Only sidebar visible.
Expected: A proper 404 page with "Page Not Found" message and a link back to the dashboard
Actual: Blank white content area. No error message. No redirect. URL stays at the invalid path.
Impact: Users who mistype URLs or follow broken links see nothing. No way to recover without manually navigating.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /any-invalid-url
Suggested Fix: Add a catch-all route in React Router that renders a 404 component with navigation back to dashboard.
Original Report IDs: PDF-BUG-010 | First Reported: March 5, 2026 (PDF Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-008 MEDIUM STILL EXISTS
Page: /scheduling
Scheduling Page Shows Minimal Data — Single Overdue Work Order
The Scheduling page shows only 1 work order ("Work Order - Jan 28, 4:46 PM") with an overdue indicator and "After 4/20/26" date. The calendar view for April 2026 is entirely empty except for today's date highlighted.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to Scheduling (/scheduling)
  2. Observe the Work Orders sidebar: Only 1 entry visible
  3. Check the calendar: Completely empty, no scheduled items
Expected: Calendar populated with scheduled work orders. Multiple work orders visible in the sidebar.
Actual: Single overdue work order from Jan 28. Calendar is empty. No scheduling data.
Impact: Scheduling feature is effectively non-functional. Cannot plan or view maintenance schedule.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /scheduling
Suggested Fix: Verify work order data includes scheduling dates. Check if the calendar component is filtering correctly.
Original Report IDs: PDF-BUG-030 | First Reported: March 5, 2026 (PDF Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
VER-009 MEDIUM STILL EXISTS
Page: All pages
Console Errors on Every Page — PLuG SDK, Sentry, Beamer CSP Violations
9 console errors detected on the Dashboard alone. Key errors: (1) PLuG SDK initialization fails with "Error initializing with authentication: {}" — the DevRev support widget is broken. (2) Beamer font loading violates Content Security Policy (font-src directive blocks Lato font from app.getbeamer.com). (3) Sentry envelope POST requests fail with network errors. (4) A 400 HTTP error from an API call.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to Dashboard (/dashboard)
  2. Open browser DevTools > Console tab
  3. Observe errors: "[PLuG] Error initializing with authentication: {}"
  4. Observe CSP violation: "Loading the font from app.getbeamer.com violates font-src CSP"
  5. Observe network failures: Sentry envelope POST fails
Expected: Zero console errors on page load
Actual: 9 console errors: PLuG auth failure, Beamer CSP font violation, Sentry transport errors, HTTP 400.
Impact: DevRev support widget non-functional. Error tracking (Sentry) partially broken. CSP violations indicate security policy misconfiguration.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: All pages
Suggested Fix: 1) Fix PLuG SDK auth configuration. 2) Update CSP font-src to include app.getbeamer.com. 3) Verify Sentry DSN and transport configuration. 4) Investigate the 400 API error.
Original Report IDs: HTML-BUG-001, HTML-BUG-003, HTML-BUG-014, HTML-BUG-018 | First Reported: March 21, 2026 (HTML Report) | Verified: April 20, 2026
NEW FINDINGS — Discovered During Verification (1)
VER-011 HIGH NEW FINDING
Page: /equipment-insights
Equipment Insights Page Renders Blank (Possible Regression)
The Equipment Insights page loads completely blank — no charts, no data grid, no content at all. Only the sidebar navigation and Beamer notification are visible. The automated check detected 3 chart elements, but the visual screenshot shows nothing rendered.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Login and navigate to Equipment Insights (/equipment-insights)
  2. Wait for page to fully load (10+ seconds)
  3. Observe: Content area is entirely blank
Expected: Equipment insights dashboard with charts, data grid showing equipment types, manufacturers, models
Actual: Completely blank page. No content renders.
Impact: Equipment Insights feature is non-functional. Users cannot view equipment analytics.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /equipment-insights
Suggested Fix: Check if this page requires a specific SLD or site context. Verify the data fetching logic and error handling.
Original Report IDs: New | First Reported: NEW — April 20, 2026 | Verified: April 20, 2026
IMPROVED — Partial Fix / Progress Made (1)
VER-010 LOW IMPROVED
Page: /arc-flash
Arc Flash Readiness Improved but Engineering Approved Still 0%
Arc Flash Readiness has significantly improved since the original report. Asset Details: 70% (was 2%). Source/Target Connections: 8%. Connection Details: 29%. However, Engineering Approved remains at 0%. Individual equipment: Busway 100%, Circuit Breaker 97%, Fuse 0%, Generator 100%.
Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Navigate to Arc Flash Readiness (/arc-flash)
  2. Observe the four readiness gauges
  3. Check individual equipment categories below
Expected: Readiness percentages approaching 100% across all categories
Actual: Asset Details: 70%, Source/Target: 8%, Connection Details: 29%, Engineering Approved: 0%. Fuse equipment at 0%.
Impact: Arc Flash compliance still incomplete. Engineering Approved at 0% means no connections have been approved.
Screenshot captured: April 20, 2026 | Page: /arc-flash
Suggested Fix: Focus on completing Fuse equipment data and engineering approval workflow.
